name: Ostrobothnia (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the gules field of the historic Ostrobothnian arms
the golden upper stripe and the golden charges of the arms

description: A square flag with a narrow yellow stripe over a wide red field, charged with the gold provincial arms of Ostrobothnia.
history: Ostrobothnia (Pohjanmaa) became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, derived from the historic provincial arms of the broader Ostrobothnian coast.

features.symbolism: the historic provincial arms of Ostrobothnia: six golden ermine spots arranged 3-2-1, with a crowned arm grasping an arrow above. The arms refer to the medieval ermine-fur trade of the Ostrobothnian coast.
